summary: The agent concludes based on developmental anatomy and eruption timing that root calcification in deciduous teeth is primarily completed by approximately four years of age. This conclusion follows logically since primary teeth erupt after crowning is done (~ages 2–3) and are usually shed between six and seven years—indicating their roots were sufficiently formed prior to this period.

Answer choice: B
